pi_items.verified_value: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023)
pi_items.source: GitHub release: https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2 and install script URL: https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/download/v5.2.2/openbcigui_v5.2.2_2023-08-21_16-14-34_linux64.zip

pi_items.metadata_value: PhysioNet EEGMMI Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), Subject S001, Run R01 (eyes open baseline)
pi_items.verified_value: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset version 1.0.0, Subject S001, Run R01 (baseline eyes-open condition), 1 minute duration, 64 channels at 160 Hz
pi_items.source: PhysioNet EEGMMI dataset: https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/ and dataset documentation describing baseline runs

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated in metadata, but original PhysioNet data has 64 channels
pi_items.verified_value: 64 channels (per PhysioNet EEGMMI specifications: https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/)
pi_items.source: PhysioNet EEGMMI dataset specifications and conversion script in install_openbci.sh

pi_items.verified_value: 8 (converted from original 64 channels via channel selection in conversion script)
pi_items.source: install_openbci.sh lines 208-272 showing conversion from 64 channels to 8 selected channels (AF3/F3/FC5, AF4/F4/FC6, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2 then mapped to Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2)

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated in metadata, but original PhysioNet data is at 160 Hz
pi_items.verified_value: 160 Hz (per PhysioNet EEGMMI specifications)
pi_items.source: PhysioNet documentation: https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/ states '64-channel EEGs, each sampled at 160 samples per second'

pi_items.verified_value: 250 (resampled from original 160 Hz via scipy.signal.resample in conversion script)
pi_items.source: install_openbci.sh lines 292-298 showing resampling: 'from scipy.signal import resample' with target_srate=250

pi_items.verified_value: ["Fp1", "Fp2", "C3", "C4", "P7", "P8", "O1", "O2"] - mapped OpenBCI standard names for the 8 selected channels
pi_items.source: install_openbci.sh line 272: 'openbci_names = ['Fp1', 'Fp2', 'C3', 'C4', 'P7', 'P8', 'O1', 'O2']'

pi_items.verified_value: Both Band Power and FFT Plot widgets exist in OpenBCI GUI v5.2 and support playback mode
pi_items.source: OpenBCI GUI Widget Guide: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/ and OpenBCI Forum discussion of these widgets working together

pi_items.verified_value: 1.0 Hz (bandpass filter configurable in OpenBCI GUI; default is 5-50Hz but 1-50Hz is standard for full EEG spectrum analysis)
pi_items.source: OpenBCI forum post 'About filters in GUI' confirms bandpass is configurable; installation script and typical EEG practice supports 1-50Hz full spectrum range

pi_items.verified_value: 50.0 Hz (bandpass filter configurable in OpenBCI GUI; 50Hz is the standard upper frequency limit)
pi_items.source: OpenBCI documentation and GUI Widget Guide: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/ showing 5-50Hz as default with configurability

pi_items.verified_value: PLAYBACK - file-based playback from recorded EEG data
pi_items.source: Task description and setup scripts consistently reference playback mode and file loading from ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/

pi_items.verified_value: OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt (generated from PhysioNet S001R01.edf via conversion script)
pi_items.source: install_openbci.sh line 142 and lines 349-354 showing conversion of S001R01.edf to OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt

pi_items.verified_value: 8-12 Hz is standard alpha wave frequency range in neuroscience
pi_items.source: Standard neuroscience/EEG literature and task description document

pi_items.metadata_value: O1 (channel 7) and O2 (channel 8) mentioned in task description as best for alpha visibility
pi_items.verified_value: O1 and O2 are occipital (back of head) electrodes where alpha waves are most prominent; using 1-based indexing O1=channel 7, O2=channel 8 (positions 6 and 7 in 0-based array)
pi_items.source: Neuroscience literature on alpha wave topography and channel_order array in task metadata (positions 6 and 7 = 1-indexed channels 7 and 8)

pi_items.metadata_value: ~61 seconds (mentioned in README as 'Running resting-state...61 seconds')
pi_items.verified_value: Nominally 60 seconds (one-minute baseline run per EEGMMI spec); 61 seconds is approximately one minute with minor variation
pi_items.source: PhysioNet EEGMMI documentation: https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/ specifies 'two one-minute baseline runs'

pi_items.verified_value: Expert Mode is a feature in OpenBCI GUI that enables screenshot capture via 'm' key
pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh documentation mention Expert Mode and 'm' key for screenshots

pi_items.verified_value: Screenshot capture required via 'm' key in Expert Mode
pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py require new screenshots to be captured

pi_items.verified_value: Settings must be saved to a configuration file
pi_items.source: Verifier.py checks for settings_file with modification timestamp after task start

pi_items.verified_value: 100 µV (microvolts) is specified as the vertical scale for Time Series widget
pi_items.source: Task metadata and export_result.sh parsing looks for timeseries_scale_uv setting

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated in task metadata
pi_items.verified_value: PhysioNet Credentialed Health Data License - publicly available
pi_items.source: install_openbci.sh lines 136-137: 'License: PhysioNet Credentialed Health Data License — publicly available'

pi_items.metadata_value: International 10-10 system implied by standard electrode names
pi_items.verified_value: International 10-10 system (64 electrodes excluding Nz, F9, F10, FT9, FT10, A1, A2, TP9, TP10, P9, P10)
pi_items.source: PhysioNet EEGMMI documentation confirms use of international 10-10 electrode system

privileged_info_summary: The eyes_open_alpha_analysis task uses real human EEG data from PhysioNet EEGMMI Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), Subject S001, Run R01—a 60-second eyes-open baseline recording originally captured at 160 Hz with 64 channels using the international 10-10 electrode system. The data has been converted to OpenBCI format: resampled to 250 Hz and reduced to 8 channels (Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2). The task requires OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023) configured in playback mode with Band Power and FFT Plot widgets visible, bandpass filter set to 1-50 Hz, and alpha waves (8-12 Hz) should be visible over occipital channels O1 and O2. The data is non-synthetic, sourced from a publicly available research dataset with proper PhysioNet licensing.
